title: The 10 Highest-Grossing Movies of 2025, Ranked
text:
Top 10 highest-grossing movies of 2025 ranked by worldwide box office:

1. Ne Zha 2 - $2,150,000,000. This Chinese fantasy sequel dominated the global box office by nearly a billion dollars. Released during Chinese New Year, the film follows Ne Zha and Ao Bing as they battle Master Shen. Director Jiaozi made it the most expensive production to date at 600 million yuan. The film adapted the 16th-century novel Investiture of the Gods and incorporated themes of family and friendship, earning universal praise for its visuals and adventure storytelling.

2. Zootopia 2 - $1,464,212,160. Judy Hopps and Nick Wilde return in this animated crime comedy as they pursue a reptilian villain while clearing their names. The film became the second-highest-grossing film of the year, blending pop culture references with real-world themes about marginalized communities. Its success launched it into the ranks of the seventh-biggest animated films ever.

3. Lilo & Stitch - $1,038,027,526. Disney's live-action adaptation found critical and commercial success after concerns following Snow White's reception. The film maintains the core message that ohana means family while featuring stellar CGI and warmth. Despite significant changes from the original, it resonated with families and Disney enthusiasts alike.

4. A Minecraft Movie - $958,149,195. Four misfits are transported into a cubic world and must find their way home with help from Steve (Jack Black). While critics were mixed, audiences embraced the campy good time. The film generated viral moments, particularly among Gen Z viewers, securing a 2027 sequel.

5. Jurassic World: Rebirth - $869,146,189. A covert team seeks DNA from three colossal dinosaurs on an abandoned island, encountering complications when a civilian family appears. The film proved successful internationally, setting franchise records in India and performing strongly in China, the UK, Mexico, and Germany.

6. Avatar: Fire and Ash - $851,695,254. James Cameron's third installment explores Jake Sully and Neytiri navigating grief while encountering an aggressive fire-worshipping Na'vi tribe. The film examines family, loss, and a society's destructive path through volcanic landscapes and introduces plot points affecting future entries.

7. Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba — The Movie: Infinity Castle - $718,473,620. This R-rated animated film from Japan became the first non-American animated film to gross over the $700 million mark. The epic follows the Demon Slayer Corps entering Muzan Kibutsuji's stronghold for their final battle, earning acclaim for action sequences and visuals.

8. How to Train Your Dragon - $636,351,148. Director Dean DeBlois brings the 2010 animated film to live-action, following Hiccup's bond with Toothless the dragon. The adaptation doesn't necessarily reinvent the wheel but enhances the viewing experience for both longtime fans and new audiences.

9. F1: The Movie - $631,627,111. Directed by Joseph Kosinski, this film stars Brad Pitt as a retired Formula 1 driver mentoring a young rookie. Apple Studios' first box-office success blended realistic racing action with underdog sports themes, appealing to both F1 enthusiasts and general audiences.

10. Superman - $616,784,465. James Gunn's DCU launch stars David Corenswet as the Man of Steel, balancing Kryptonian heritage with human identity. The film brought the signature Gunn superhero essence to a beloved franchise, earning a 2027 sequel titled Man of Tomorrow.
